signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

heroMedia.title: Kevin Yebo, Chemnitz: 'I can't wait for the playoffs'
heroMedia.summary: The Chemnitz forward opens up on pressure free basketball, his national team stint, and the joy behind his YouTube storytelling

linkedIds.text: Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana v NINERS Chemnitz (2026-03-11T18:00:00.000Z)

content.title: 2025-26 EuroCup Regular Season highlights: Kevin Yebo, Chemnitz
content.description: Kevin Yebo if NINERS Chemnitz is in his first BKT EuroCup season.

content.text:
**Do you think that having less pressure can be a factor, especially if the game is close?**

"We know that they are a really experienced team in Europe. They have been there for a long time. I know they will be prepared, for sure. Our biggest strength is that we have no pressure. We have to go there and play free. Everybody knows that if you play free, you play your best basketball. That's what they have to be ready for. We have to be ready for their experience on this level. I think it's going to be a great game. Two different energies clashing. We will see who advances to the next round."

 

**Your teammate Amadou Sow played for Cedevita two years ago. Is that an advantage, having a player who knows some of your opponents well?**

"I think so, yes. He is going to be ready. Like you said, he played there. He knows the gym and everything. Maybe he can be someone who walks in front. We can see how he is already confident or comfortable in this court, in this environment. Maybe we can take some part of his energy. That is helpful for sure. He can tell us something about the opponent and help us to prepare for the team. That's helpful, for sure."

 

**You have your own [YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@tobecontinued53) with really well-produced videos. Where did the idea come from, a YouTube channel with such quality content?**

"It's been fun. I've got some friends who cut videos professionally. I like to be creative with something other than basketball. I like to experiment on other stuff and will keep doing that. I want to make some videos to inspire and prepare the next generation for what they have to be prepared for. They want to live this basketball life. I want to give the new generation some inspiration and some wisdom. It gives me a lot of joy. If you have achieved a lot of your own dreams, then you have to help the next generation to achieve their dreams. This YouTube channel is the beginning of a lot of wisdom for the next generation. First of all, I think the life of a basketball player is really quick. Things happen really quick, especially with two games a week. Sometimes I get the feeling that one or two weeks in our life is like what happens in two months for other people. So to do these videos and to also talk about different experiences I 've had helps me to relive these moments. And enjoy them in a little bit slower environment. And also, like I said, to share it with others and give them insights. For me, it's healing to do this stuff."

<center><iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/oR-uI3Oa-VI?si=uIcvYqmtMPVkUvSC" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe></center>

**I wanted to ask you about your nicknames, JoyBoy and Kazaam. Where did these two nicknames come from?**

"Kazaam is a movie. Shaquille O'Neal was in this movie, he is like a genie out of a bottle. It was one of my favorite movies when I grew up as a child. When I was 16, when I started playing basketball, I realized that the actor was a basketball player, so I decided to give me the nickname Kazaam. And also JoyBoy is from my favorite anime show, One Piece. JoyBoy is the main character. He's like a pirate. He's on adventures. JoyBoy is like a type of energy he is bringing to the world just by enjoying life. That's what I want to do too. It is inspiring to see this anime. These are two nicknames that describe a little bit of my character."

 

**This is your first EuroCup season after playing in the EuroLeague with Bayern. How did you like it so far?**

"I really like the competition. It's physical. I like to play physical. The refs are good. The refs do a really good job of allowing a certain physicality without crossing a line of craziness. Also, I like the competition. Every game counts. It makes every game a high-quality one. That's what I really like. I like the whole organization of the EuroCup. It's really professional on and off the court. I really enjoyed the regular season and I can't wait for the playoffs."

 

**This is your second stint in Chemnitz, which shows that you really like there. Does that make this great run even more special?**

"Yes, of course. I didn't plan to do all this, but I'm really thankful that everything happened like this. I decided to come back because I thought it was the best thing to do. When I found out that we were playing the EuroCup this season, there was one thing I really looked forward to, playing the first EuroCup season with this team. And that we managed to go to the playoffs in the first season. I think that's something really special and I'm really thankful to be a part of this. It's not over and I can't wait. Like I said, we all can't wait for the next game. We know that the city is behind us and everybody is already proud of us. So like I said, we'll go there and play free because we have nothing to lose. We are in the best situation we could be."

articleMetadata.description: In a conversation with Javier Gancedo, Chemnitz forward Kevin Yebo reflects on the club’s first EuroCup season and its unexpected run to the Playoffs. Despite injuries and the demands of competing on two fronts, Yebo says the team “found a way” and now embraces its underdog status ahead of a do or die road game at Cedevita. Fresh off World Cup qualifiers with Ivory Coast, he emphasizes playing “free,” leaning on team energy, and drawing insight from teammate Amadou Sow’s knowledge of Ljubljana. Yebo also discusses his creative YouTube project, inspired by a desire to guide the next generation and relive his own experiences “in a slower environment.”

heroMedia.title: Buducnost battles to banish its Eighthfinals hex
heroMedia.summary: After years of heartbreak at this stage, the Montenegrin champions face red‑hot Cluj in a decisive showdown at Moraca

content.text:
The BKT EuroCup Regular Season wrapped in early February and - after 18 intense rounds, the field was trimmed to 12 contenders. The top two teams from each group punched direct tickets to the Quarterfinals, while the remaining eight now face high‑stakes Eighthfinals showdowns — one game, win or go home, with a place in the top eight on the line. Among those fighting for survival is Buducnost VOLI Podgorica, a club hungry to extend its journey.

Coach Andrej Zakelj's charges placed third in Group B with a 12-6 record and earned the right to host U‑BT Cluj‑Napoca - a familiar foe from the ABA League-  in Podgorica. The Montenegrin champions now stand just one victory away from their first EuroCup Quarterfinal berth since the 2020–21 season.

Since that last playoff appearance, Buducnost has stumbled at this phase - excluding 2023-24 when it failed to even advance from the group stage. That history adds extra weight to Wednesday’s showdown. The blue‑and‑white will look to break the cycle, banish the lingering eighthfinals curse, and seize a long‑awaited return to the EuroCup’s elite eight, where mighty Bahcesehir College Istanbul awaits. 

Read more [HERE](https://www.eurohoops.net/en/trademarks/1941823/buducnost-battles-to-banish-its-eighthfinals-hex/).

content.text:
The 2025–26 BKT EuroCup season resumes with the playoffs, and the eighthfinals bring four do‑or‑die games – all played on Wednesday, which naturally affects the [EuroCup Fantasy Challenge](https://euroleaguefantasy.euroleaguebasketball.net/11). 

There’s no deep strategy here: simply pick the six players you trust most, choose a winning coach, and you should be in good shape. Unlimited trades are also available before each round. I’m here to help by highlighting one player from each matchup, plus a sleeper pick I like a lot.

content.title: Fantasy tips: EuroCup Eighthfinals

content.text:
### Mitch Creek, U‑BT Cluj‑Napoca (16.0 credits)

[Mitch Creek](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/mitchell-creek/013927/) has been outstanding all season, scoring inside and out, rebounding, and providing the toughness and energy you expect from an Australian forward. Players like him live for elimination games, so expect his best version here. Make him your captain, too.

 

### Kyle Alexander, Turk Telekom Ankara (14.6 credits)

Turk Telekom has a size advantage in the paint and should rely on it. [Kyle Alexander](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/kyle-alexander/011973/) is one of the team’s most experienced players and should play a vital role. His dunks, rebounds, and rim protection give him a very high floor. A rock‑solid choice.

 

### Umoja Gibson, Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana (12.7 credits)

[Umoja Gibson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/umoja-gibson/013855/) enters the eighthfinals in excellent form, coming off a standout Adriatic League game against Dubai with 20 points, 8 assists and 4 steals for a PIR of 31 in a win. He should carry that momentum into a do‑or‑die home game in which Cedevita is the heavy favorite.

 

### Jordan Parks, Umana Reyer Venice (10.6 credits)

The Italian matchup is tricky to predict, but [Jordan Parks](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/jordan-parks/011909/) is trending upward. He just had 21 points and 6 rebounds for a PIR of 29 — plus the winning free throws — in a 90–91 road victory in Treviso. Earlier in the season, he posted a PIR of 20 against Trento in Italian League play. Parks is a strong, steady option.

 

### Pierre Oriola, BAXI Manresa (9.7 credits)

[Pierre Oriola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/pierre-oriola/005917/) has been sensational, earning a return to the Spanish national team thanks to his recent form. In his last EuroCup game, he put up 14 points and a PIR of 23 against Hamburg. Oriola's most recent Spanish League outing featured 13 points and a PIR of 22. He could be your X‑factor.

heroMedia.title: EuroCup experiencing Cedevita Olimpija's Umoja Gibson in MMMP mode
heroMedia.summary: The first-year guard has taken the competition by storm this season

content.text:
[Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/teams/cedevita-olimpija-ljubljana/lju/) knows that when it needs a basket, [Umoja Gibson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/umoja-gibson/013855/) can deliver. All the BKT EuroCup debutant needs to do is go into MMMP mode. And then the opponent is going to have Mo Money, Mo Problems.

Gibson has helped Cedevita Olimpija charge to the EuroCup Playoffs with 16.3 points per game to go with 2.2 rebounds, 4.7 assists and 1.5 steals. He poured in 25 points in back-to-back games against Bahcesehir College Istanbul and Neptunas Klaipeda and posted a season-high PIR of 31 in the final game against Slask Wroclaw with 20 points and 9 assists.

"When you're on the court scoring points, back home we call it getting to the money. And then I am calling more problems to my opponents. So it's Mo Money, Mo Problems," Gibson, who goes by the nickname Mo, told David Hein. "If Mo gets going, you guys have more problems."

Gibson was given the MMMP moniker during high school by an AAU coach. "I just took it and ran with it."

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Umoja Gibson (@mo.gib)

It shouldn't surprise that Gibson has excelled in his first EuroCup campaign, especially considering his mental approach.

"My mindset coming into this year was to be who I am, be confident," he said. "It was a step up, so my main objective was to be confident in myself and produce at the highest level I can."

Gibson has loved the jump from Spartak Subotica to Cedevita Olimpija and the EuroCup.

"I was overly excited. I'm the type of player who loves competition. I thrive off high-level competition – it brings the best out of me, playing against elite players."

Gibson's work ethic stems from having to fight for everything back home in East Waco, Texas. Growing up, he played pickup games against his older brothers and neighborhood kids who didn't go easy on him.

"They didn't treat me like a regular younger person. They used to treat me rough, pushing me down, blocking my shot, making it tough on me. But I always figured out a way to score and facilitate," he recalled.

Space was another luxury Gibson had to fight for. After his father was incarcerated, Gibson and his family moved into his grandmother's apartment – eight or nine people in a one-and-a-half bedroom unit.

"My favorite memory was waking up to breakfast from my granny. She had pancakes and eggs. Just waking up to that smell and walking into the kitchen to see my granny smiling and saying good morning."

content.title: Umoja Gibson's top plays in 2025-26
content.description: The best plays of Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana guard Umoja Gibson in the 2025-26 BKT EuroCup regular season.

content.text:
Gibson credits his mentor [Tweety Carter](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/demond-carter/004398/) for helping him excel in Europe from the start.

"He gave me a lot of pointers on what to expect – the mentality and mindset you have to have to be here," he said of Carter, who played at Baylor University before a 11-year European career that included EuroCup seasons with Cibona Zagreb and Nymburk.

Gibson's professional debut came at German club BG Gottingen in the FIBA Europe Cup, which provided valuable international experience. The demanding two-game weeks taught him about European basketball while also exposing him to the mental toll of frequent travel.

His appreciation for European basketball deepened last season with Spartak Subotica in Serbia.

"Serbians are very passionate people. They love basketball – even in the small city I was in, Subotica. They know the sport," he said.

Gibson helped Spartak reach the ABA League playoffs in its inaugural season, though they fell to Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade in the semifinals. Spartak also faced Partizan in the Serbian League finals after getting past Crvena Meridianbet Zvezda in the semis. Gibson had returned home after his contract expired but came back for the finals after Spartak re-signed him.

"Same apartment, same everything."

Gibson averaged 6.5 points, 2.0 rebounds, and 1.0 assists in the Serbian League finals, but Partizan took the title 2-0. Still, Gibson had shown he could perform in big moments – those moments when he goes into MMMP mode.

heroMedia.title: Cedevita Olimpija extends coach Zvezdan Mitrovic
heroMedia.summary: He signed a new deal until the end of the 2027-28 season

content.text:
Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana announced a new agreement with head coach Zvezdan Mitrovic to keep working together until the end of the 2027-28 season. Mitrovic, who is a former EuroCup champion after guiding AS Monaco to the BKT EuroCup championship in 2021, is in his second season with the club from the Slovenian capital.

So far, Mitrovic helped Cedevita win all possible domestic titles: back-to-back Slovenian Cup and Slovenian Supercup crowns, as well as the Slovenian League title in 2025. This season, the 56-year old Mitrovic has led the team to the EuroCup eighthfinals where they face U-BT Cluj-Napoca next week. Cedevita posted a 12-6 record and won a third place in Group A.

✍️ Zvezdan Mitrović stays with us until 2028!

The head coach of Cedevita Olimpija has extended his contract with the club until the end of the 2027/28 season.

Together we continue our journey. 💚🧡#ZmajevaDruzina pic.twitter.com/UnDetQJEpu

— Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana - English (@KKCedOL_EN) March 5, 2026

Mitrovic started his head coaching career in Ukraine, with Khimik Yuzhny, Kryvbasbasket Kryvyi, Budivelunik Kiev and Azovmash Mariupol before joining Monaco in January 2015. He helped the team promote to the French elite later on that season, and went on to win three consecutive French Leaders Cup titles from 2016 to 2018. Mitrovic joined LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne, leading it to the French League title in 2019.  He returned to Monaco, where he led it all the way in the EuroCup after sweeping UNICS Kazan in the best-of-three finals. Mitrovic later coached Galatasaray Istanbul of Türkiye before joining Cedevita. He has also coached the Montenegrin national team, helping it quality to the 2019 World Cup.

"Actually, the decision was very natural. As a coach, I have very good working conditions here. It’s a healthy and encouraging environment – from the club management and the president all the way to the bus driver. Great chemistry has developed among us. We have achieved a nice result, and at the same time I feel good in Ljubljana and Slovenia in my private life as well. I feel a sense of belonging and I see room for progress and development, which we are systematically working on," Mitrovic said. "I am proud that we managed to build a real team. I’m not talking only about the twelve players on the court, but about the entire team and the coaching staff. There is no division between top and bottom with us – we are all one."

heroMedia.title: Euroleague Basketball approves alternative venues for Israeli and Dubai teams' home games

content.text:
Euroleague Basketball has approved alternative venues for Israeli and Dubai teams to play the remaining of the 2025-26 EuroLeague Regular Season and the BKT EuroCup home games as per the current situation in their region.

- Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv will host all its home games in Serbia's capital, Belgrade at Aleksandar Nikolic Hall.
- Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv will play their home games in Bulgaria’s capital, Sofia, at Arena 8888.
- Dubai Basketball will play their home games in Bosnia & Herzegovina’s capital, Sarajevo, at Zetra Arena.
- Hapoel Midtown Jersualem will host all its home games in Serbia's capital, Belgrade at Ranko Zeravica Sports Hall.

Euroleague Basketball will continue to monitor the evolution of the situation, while staying in contact with all related parties, and will announce any modifications regarding the above decisions in the case that the situation in the region changes.

Euroleague Basketball is currently evaluating the best options to reschedule and/or relocate the games involving teams affected by the suspended Round 21 and Round 30 matchups. Further information will be released in the coming days.

heroMedia.title: Buducnost battles to banish its Eighthfinals hex
heroMedia.summary: After years of heartbreak at this stage, the Montenegrin champions face red‑hot Cluj in a decisive showdown at Moraca

content.text:
The BKT EuroCup Regular Season wrapped in early February and - after 18 intense rounds, the field was trimmed to 12 contenders. The top two teams from each group punched direct tickets to the Quarterfinals, while the remaining eight now face high‑stakes Eighthfinals showdowns — one game, win or go home, with a place in the top eight on the line. Among those fighting for survival is Buducnost VOLI Podgorica, a club hungry to extend its journey.

Coach Andrej Zakelj's charges placed third in Group B with a 12-6 record and earned the right to host U‑BT Cluj‑Napoca - a familiar foe from the ABA League-  in Podgorica. The Montenegrin champions now stand just one victory away from their first EuroCup Quarterfinal berth since the 2020–21 season.

Since that last playoff appearance, Buducnost has stumbled at this phase - excluding 2023-24 when it failed to even advance from the group stage. That history adds extra weight to Wednesday’s showdown. The blue‑and‑white will look to break the cycle, banish the lingering eighthfinals curse, and seize a long‑awaited return to the EuroCup’s elite eight, where mighty Bahcesehir College Istanbul awaits. 

Read more [HERE](https://www.eurohoops.net/en/trademarks/1941823/buducnost-battles-to-banish-its-eighthfinals-hex/).

heroMedia.title: Javier Gancedo’s fantasy tips for the EuroCup Eighthfinals
heroMedia.summary: From Mitch Creek’s toughness to Umoja Gibson’s momentum, Javier Gancedo picks his favorite fantasy targets for the opening playoff round

linkedIds.text: Umana Reyer Venice v Dolomiti Energia Trento (2026-03-11T19:00:00.000Z)
Buducnost VOLI Podgorica v U-BT Cluj-Napoca (2026-03-11T18:00:00.000Z)
Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana v NINERS Chemnitz (2026-03-11T18:00:00.000Z)
Turk Telekom Ankara v BAXI Manresa (2026-03-11T17:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
The 2025–26 BKT EuroCup season resumes with the playoffs, and the eighthfinals bring four do‑or‑die games – all played on Wednesday, which naturally affects the [EuroCup Fantasy Challenge](https://euroleaguefantasy.euroleaguebasketball.net/11). 

There’s no deep strategy here: simply pick the six players you trust most, choose a winning coach, and you should be in good shape. Unlimited trades are also available before each round. I’m here to help by highlighting one player from each matchup, plus a sleeper pick I like a lot.

content.title: Fantasy tips: EuroCup Eighthfinals

content.text:
### Mitch Creek, U‑BT Cluj‑Napoca (16.0 credits)

[Mitch Creek](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/mitchell-creek/013927/) has been outstanding all season, scoring inside and out, rebounding, and providing the toughness and energy you expect from an Australian forward. Players like him live for elimination games, so expect his best version here. Make him your captain, too.

 

### Kyle Alexander, Turk Telekom Ankara (14.6 credits)

Turk Telekom has a size advantage in the paint and should rely on it. [Kyle Alexander](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/kyle-alexander/011973/) is one of the team’s most experienced players and should play a vital role. His dunks, rebounds, and rim protection give him a very high floor. A rock‑solid choice.

 

### Umoja Gibson, Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana (12.7 credits)

[Umoja Gibson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/umoja-gibson/013855/) enters the eighthfinals in excellent form, coming off a standout Adriatic League game against Dubai with 20 points, 8 assists and 4 steals for a PIR of 31 in a win. He should carry that momentum into a do‑or‑die home game in which Cedevita is the heavy favorite.

 

### Jordan Parks, Umana Reyer Venice (10.6 credits)

The Italian matchup is tricky to predict, but [Jordan Parks](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/jordan-parks/011909/) is trending upward. He just had 21 points and 6 rebounds for a PIR of 29 — plus the winning free throws — in a 90–91 road victory in Treviso. Earlier in the season, he posted a PIR of 20 against Trento in Italian League play. Parks is a strong, steady option.

 

### Pierre Oriola, BAXI Manresa (9.7 credits)

[Pierre Oriola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/pierre-oriola/005917/) has been sensational, earning a return to the Spanish national team thanks to his recent form. In his last EuroCup game, he put up 14 points and a PIR of 23 against Hamburg. Oriola's most recent Spanish League outing featured 13 points and a PIR of 22. He could be your X‑factor.

heroMedia.title: EuroCup experiencing Cedevita Olimpija's Umoja Gibson in MMMP mode
heroMedia.summary: The first-year guard has taken the competition by storm this season

content.text:
[Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/teams/cedevita-olimpija-ljubljana/lju/) knows that when it needs a basket, [Umoja Gibson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/umoja-gibson/013855/) can deliver. All the BKT EuroCup debutant needs to do is go into MMMP mode. And then the opponent is going to have Mo Money, Mo Problems.

Gibson has helped Cedevita Olimpija charge to the EuroCup Playoffs with 16.3 points per game to go with 2.2 rebounds, 4.7 assists and 1.5 steals. He poured in 25 points in back-to-back games against Bahcesehir College Istanbul and Neptunas Klaipeda and posted a season-high PIR of 31 in the final game against Slask Wroclaw with 20 points and 9 assists.

"When you're on the court scoring points, back home we call it getting to the money. And then I am calling more problems to my opponents. So it's Mo Money, Mo Problems," Gibson, who goes by the nickname Mo, told David Hein. "If Mo gets going, you guys have more problems."

Gibson was given the MMMP moniker during high school by an AAU coach. "I just took it and ran with it."

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Umoja Gibson (@mo.gib)

It shouldn't surprise that Gibson has excelled in his first EuroCup campaign, especially considering his mental approach.

"My mindset coming into this year was to be who I am, be confident," he said. "It was a step up, so my main objective was to be confident in myself and produce at the highest level I can."

Gibson has loved the jump from Spartak Subotica to Cedevita Olimpija and the EuroCup.

"I was overly excited. I'm the type of player who loves competition. I thrive off high-level competition – it brings the best out of me, playing against elite players."

Gibson's work ethic stems from having to fight for everything back home in East Waco, Texas. Growing up, he played pickup games against his older brothers and neighborhood kids who didn't go easy on him.

"They didn't treat me like a regular younger person. They used to treat me rough, pushing me down, blocking my shot, making it tough on me. But I always figured out a way to score and facilitate," he recalled.

Space was another luxury Gibson had to fight for. After his father was incarcerated, Gibson and his family moved into his grandmother's apartment – eight or nine people in a one-and-a-half bedroom unit.

"My favorite memory was waking up to breakfast from my granny. She had pancakes and eggs. Just waking up to that smell and walking into the kitchen to see my granny smiling and saying good morning."

content.title: Umoja Gibson's top plays in 2025-26
content.description: The best plays of Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana guard Umoja Gibson in the 2025-26 BKT EuroCup regular season.

content.text:
Gibson credits his mentor [Tweety Carter](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/demond-carter/004398/) for helping him excel in Europe from the start.

"He gave me a lot of pointers on what to expect – the mentality and mindset you have to have to be here," he said of Carter, who played at Baylor University before a 11-year European career that included EuroCup seasons with Cibona Zagreb and Nymburk.

Gibson's professional debut came at German club BG Gottingen in the FIBA Europe Cup, which provided valuable international experience. The demanding two-game weeks taught him about European basketball while also exposing him to the mental toll of frequent travel.

His appreciation for European basketball deepened last season with Spartak Subotica in Serbia.

"Serbians are very passionate people. They love basketball – even in the small city I was in, Subotica. They know the sport," he said.

Gibson helped Spartak reach the ABA League playoffs in its inaugural season, though they fell to Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade in the semifinals. Spartak also faced Partizan in the Serbian League finals after getting past Crvena Meridianbet Zvezda in the semis. Gibson had returned home after his contract expired but came back for the finals after Spartak re-signed him.

"Same apartment, same everything."

Gibson averaged 6.5 points, 2.0 rebounds, and 1.0 assists in the Serbian League finals, but Partizan took the title 2-0. Still, Gibson had shown he could perform in big moments – those moments when he goes into MMMP mode.

heroMedia.title: Cedevita Olimpija extends coach Zvezdan Mitrovic
heroMedia.summary: He signed a new deal until the end of the 2027-28 season

content.text:
Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana announced a new agreement with head coach Zvezdan Mitrovic to keep working together until the end of the 2027-28 season. Mitrovic, who is a former EuroCup champion after guiding AS Monaco to the BKT EuroCup championship in 2021, is in his second season with the club from the Slovenian capital.

So far, Mitrovic helped Cedevita win all possible domestic titles: back-to-back Slovenian Cup and Slovenian Supercup crowns, as well as the Slovenian League title in 2025. This season, the 56-year old Mitrovic has led the team to the EuroCup eighthfinals where they face U-BT Cluj-Napoca next week. Cedevita posted a 12-6 record and won a third place in Group A.

✍️ Zvezdan Mitrović stays with us until 2028!

The head coach of Cedevita Olimpija has extended his contract with the club until the end of the 2027/28 season.

Together we continue our journey. 💚🧡#ZmajevaDruzina pic.twitter.com/UnDetQJEpu

— Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana - English (@KKCedOL_EN) March 5, 2026

Mitrovic started his head coaching career in Ukraine, with Khimik Yuzhny, Kryvbasbasket Kryvyi, Budivelunik Kiev and Azovmash Mariupol before joining Monaco in January 2015. He helped the team promote to the French elite later on that season, and went on to win three consecutive French Leaders Cup titles from 2016 to 2018. Mitrovic joined LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne, leading it to the French League title in 2019.  He returned to Monaco, where he led it all the way in the EuroCup after sweeping UNICS Kazan in the best-of-three finals. Mitrovic later coached Galatasaray Istanbul of Türkiye before joining Cedevita. He has also coached the Montenegrin national team, helping it quality to the 2019 World Cup.

"Actually, the decision was very natural. As a coach, I have very good working conditions here. It’s a healthy and encouraging environment – from the club management and the president all the way to the bus driver. Great chemistry has developed among us. We have achieved a nice result, and at the same time I feel good in Ljubljana and Slovenia in my private life as well. I feel a sense of belonging and I see room for progress and development, which we are systematically working on," Mitrovic said. "I am proud that we managed to build a real team. I’m not talking only about the twelve players on the court, but about the entire team and the coaching staff. There is no division between top and bottom with us – we are all one."

heroMedia.title: Euroleague Basketball approves alternative venues for Israeli and Dubai teams' home games

content.text:
Euroleague Basketball has approved alternative venues for Israeli and Dubai teams to play the remaining of the 2025-26 EuroLeague Regular Season and the BKT EuroCup home games as per the current situation in their region.

- Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv will host all its home games in Serbia's capital, Belgrade at Aleksandar Nikolic Hall.
- Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv will play their home games in Bulgaria’s capital, Sofia, at Arena 8888.
- Dubai Basketball will play their home games in Bosnia & Herzegovina’s capital, Sarajevo, at Zetra Arena.
- Hapoel Midtown Jersualem will host all its home games in Serbia's capital, Belgrade at Ranko Zeravica Sports Hall.

Euroleague Basketball will continue to monitor the evolution of the situation, while staying in contact with all related parties, and will announce any modifications regarding the above decisions in the case that the situation in the region changes.

Euroleague Basketball is currently evaluating the best options to reschedule and/or relocate the games involving teams affected by the suspended Round 21 and Round 30 matchups. Further information will be released in the coming days.

heroMedia.title: Kevin Yebo, Chemnitz: 'I can't wait for the playoffs'
heroMedia.summary: The Chemnitz forward opens up on pressure free basketball, his national team stint, and the joy behind his YouTube storytelling

linkedIds.text: Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana v NINERS Chemnitz (2026-03-11T18:00:00.000Z)

content.title: 2025-26 EuroCup Regular Season highlights: Kevin Yebo, Chemnitz
content.description: Kevin Yebo if NINERS Chemnitz is in his first BKT EuroCup season.

content.text:
**Do you think that having less pressure can be a factor, especially if the game is close?**

"We know that they are a really experienced team in Europe. They have been there for a long time. I know they will be prepared, for sure. Our biggest strength is that we have no pressure. We have to go there and play free. Everybody knows that if you play free, you play your best basketball. That's what they have to be ready for. We have to be ready for their experience on this level. I think it's going to be a great game. Two different energies clashing. We will see who advances to the next round."

 

**Your teammate Amadou Sow played for Cedevita two years ago. Is that an advantage, having a player who knows some of your opponents well?**

"I think so, yes. He is going to be ready. Like you said, he played there. He knows the gym and everything. Maybe he can be someone who walks in front. We can see how he is already confident or comfortable in this court, in this environment. Maybe we can take some part of his energy. That is helpful for sure. He can tell us something about the opponent and help us to prepare for the team. That's helpful, for sure."

 

**You have your own [YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@tobecontinued53) with really well-produced videos. Where did the idea come from, a YouTube channel with such quality content?**

"It's been fun. I've got some friends who cut videos professionally. I like to be creative with something other than basketball. I like to experiment on other stuff and will keep doing that. I want to make some videos to inspire and prepare the next generation for what they have to be prepared for. They want to live this basketball life. I want to give the new generation some inspiration and some wisdom. It gives me a lot of joy. If you have achieved a lot of your own dreams, then you have to help the next generation to achieve their dreams. This YouTube channel is the beginning of a lot of wisdom for the next generation. First of all, I think the life of a basketball player is really quick. Things happen really quick, especially with two games a week. Sometimes I get the feeling that one or two weeks in our life is like what happens in two months for other people. So to do these videos and to also talk about different experiences I 've had helps me to relive these moments. And enjoy them in a little bit slower environment. And also, like I said, to share it with others and give them insights. For me, it's healing to do this stuff."

<center><iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/oR-uI3Oa-VI?si=uIcvYqmtMPVkUvSC" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe></center>

**I wanted to ask you about your nicknames, JoyBoy and Kazaam. Where did these two nicknames come from?**

"Kazaam is a movie. Shaquille O'Neal was in this movie, he is like a genie out of a bottle. It was one of my favorite movies when I grew up as a child. When I was 16, when I started playing basketball, I realized that the actor was a basketball player, so I decided to give me the nickname Kazaam. And also JoyBoy is from my favorite anime show, One Piece. JoyBoy is the main character. He's like a pirate. He's on adventures. JoyBoy is like a type of energy he is bringing to the world just by enjoying life. That's what I want to do too. It is inspiring to see this anime. These are two nicknames that describe a little bit of my character."

 

**This is your first EuroCup season after playing in the EuroLeague with Bayern. How did you like it so far?**

"I really like the competition. It's physical. I like to play physical. The refs are good. The refs do a really good job of allowing a certain physicality without crossing a line of craziness. Also, I like the competition. Every game counts. It makes every game a high-quality one. That's what I really like. I like the whole organization of the EuroCup. It's really professional on and off the court. I really enjoyed the regular season and I can't wait for the playoffs."

 

**This is your second stint in Chemnitz, which shows that you really like there. Does that make this great run even more special?**

"Yes, of course. I didn't plan to do all this, but I'm really thankful that everything happened like this. I decided to come back because I thought it was the best thing to do. When I found out that we were playing the EuroCup this season, there was one thing I really looked forward to, playing the first EuroCup season with this team. And that we managed to go to the playoffs in the first season. I think that's something really special and I'm really thankful to be a part of this. It's not over and I can't wait. Like I said, we all can't wait for the next game. We know that the city is behind us and everybody is already proud of us. So like I said, we'll go there and play free because we have nothing to lose. We are in the best situation we could be."

articleMetadata.title: Kevin Yebo, Chemnitz: 'I can't wait for the playoffs'
articleMetadata.description: In a conversation with Javier Gancedo, Chemnitz forward Kevin Yebo reflects on the club’s first EuroCup season and its unexpected run to the Playoffs. Despite injuries and the demands of competing on two fronts, Yebo says the team “found a way” and now embraces its underdog status ahead of a do or die road game at Cedevita. Fresh off World Cup qualifiers with Ivory Coast, he emphasizes playing “free,” leaning on team energy, and drawing insight from teammate Amadou Sow’s knowledge of Ljubljana. Yebo also discusses his creative YouTube project, inspired by a desire to guide the next generation and relive his own experiences “in a slower environment.”

heroMedia.title: Round 11 fantasy tips: Uncovering value
heroMedia.summary: If you want to succeed in the EuroCup Fantasy Challenge, you need to find the hot players at the right prices. Here are five to consider this week.

content.text:
The secret to success in the [EuroCup Fantasy Challenge](https://eurocupfantasy.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/home) is maximizing value. That means getting points from your cheapest players, picking up others when they are hot and getting the superstars that you can afford. As we head into Round 11, there are players who fit those criteria available. Before you finalize your team, consider these options.  

[Kamar Baldwin, Dolomiti Energia Trento](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/kamar-baldwin/012597/) (11.0 credits) – It looks like Kamar Baldwin has found his groove. The combo-guard, who in playing his first EuroCup season, missed a pair of games early in the season and only posted a PIR of double figures once in his first five appearances. Since then, however, Baldwin has averaged 14.7 points, 5.3 rebounds, 5.7 assists and 2.0 steals for a PIR of 19.7 over his last three games. He is now the go-to guy in the Trento backcourt. 

[Sam Dekker, London Lions](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/sam-dekker/009672/) (15.1 credits) – This is a calculated risk. When healthy, Dekker is a dominant fantasy – and real life – force. Since his return from injury, Dekker has played limited minutes (12:44 per game) and as a result, his fantasy price has dropped by 0.7 credits. He played nearly double that in the British League on Friday and was in uniform for 14 more minutes in domestic action on Sunday. This week London plays Hamburg, which has allowed the most points in the EuroCup this season (96.9 ppg.). This may be the time to invest in Dekker.

content.text:
[L.J. Figueroa, ratiopharm Ulm](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/lj-figueroa/012603/) (11.8 credits) – Here are three encouraging facts about LJ Figueroa. He is coming off his best game (20 points, 23 PIR). His previous best game came against this week’s opponent, Dreamland Gran Canaria, in Round 2 (19 points, 20 PIR). His last three games have been his best three-game stretch of the season with averages of 14.7 points, 4.7 rebounds, 2.0 steals, PIR 17.3. Is it Figueroa time? 

[Marvin Jones, Buducnost VOLI Podgorica](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/marvin-jones/010248/) (9.4 credits) – Marvin Jones’s role in the Buducnost offense has grown in recent weeks. He has averaged 18.5 points and 4.0 rebounds for a PIR of 22.5 over his last two appearances. Jones has taken at least 10 shots in both of those games after not attempting more than 7 in any previous game this season. Jones struggled against this week’s opponent, Turk Telekom Ankara, in Round 2 with a PIR of -2, however, this is a home game and Jones, Buducnost and Turk Telekom are in different places today than they were in early October. 

[Jovan Kljajic, Dreamland Gran Canaria](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/jovan-kljajic/007560/) (4.6 credits) – One of the best cheap options in the game is Jovan Kljajic, who has continued to offer very good production for his price in recent weeks. Since Round 8, he has averaged 7.7 points and a PIR of 8.3. The outside shots have not been falling; Kljajic is shooting just 17.4% from downtown. Imagine what might happen if he heats up? Plus, Kljajic plays on Tuesday this week, so there is little risk.

heroMedia.title: Round 13 MVP: Rihards Lomazs, Neptunas Klaipeda

linkedIds.text: Veolia Towers Hamburg v Neptunas Klaipeda (2026-01-07T18:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
A career-best night in a winning effort saw [Rihards Lomazs](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/rihards-lomazs/005553/) of [Neptunas Klaipeda](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/teams/neptunas-klaipeda/kla/) secure MVP honors for Round 13 of the 2025-26 BKT EuroCup season.

Lomazs posted a career-high 33 points, making 6-of-9 two-pointers, 3-of-6 three-pointers and 12-of-12 free throws, as Neptunas beat Veolia Towers Hamburg 94-98. The Latvian guard also amassed 1 rebound, 6 assists, 1 steal and 6 fouls drawn. His career-best 36 PIR is the best of any player in this round. Lomazs is the second Neptunas player to earn MVP of the Round honors this season, after James Karnik was the tri-MVP in Round 6.

Hamburg’s [Ross Williams](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/ross-williams/014194/) finished with a career-best 34 PIR in his team’s defeat to Neptunas. He amassed a career-high 32 points, shooting a perfect 8-for-8 inside the arc, 5-for-12 from deep and 1-for-1 from the free-throw line, 5 rebounds, 2 assists and 5 fouls drawn.

[Austin Wiley](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/austin-wiley/013278/) of Hapoel Midtown Jerusalem had the third-best index rating in Round 13, which came in his team’s 72-115 victory at Slask Wroclaw. He recorded a career-high 31 PIR after posting 17 points on 6-of-8 two-pointers and 5-of-7 free throws, 12 rebounds, 1 assist, 1 steal, 1 block and 4 fouls drawn.

[Umoja Gibson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/umoja-gibson/013855/) of Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana had a career-best 30 PIR as his side fell 59-67 to Bahcesehir College Istanbul. He had a career-high 25 points on 4-of-5 two-pointers, 1-of-5 triples and 14-of-14 free throws, 3 rebounds, 3 assists, 3 steals, 1 block and 9 fouls drawn.

Rounding out the list of top performers in Round 13 are [Bryce Jones](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/bryce-jones/012621/) of Aris Thessaloniki Betsson and [Joe Thomasson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/joe-thomasson/012095/) of Panionios Cosmorama Travel Athens, both with a PIR of 29. Jones posted 27 points, 3 rebounds, 3 assists, 1 steal and 7 fouls drawn as Aris lost 113-99 at BAXI Manresa, while Thomasson amassed 16 points, 4 rebounds, 6 assists, 1 steal, 1 block and 5 fouls drawn in Panionios’s 97-85 win against Buducnost VOLI Podgorica.

### Individual highs: Rihards Lomazs, Neptunas, 33 points

Rihards Lomazs of Neptunas scored 33 points in Round 13, more than any other player. U-BT Cluj-Napoca star Fatts Russell leads the EuroCup in scoring with 22.2 points per game.

Three players finished with a round-high 12 rebounds: Fardaws Aimaq of Lietkabelis Panevezys, and Jerusalem duo Anthony Lamb and Austin Wiley. Wiley is also the season leader in rebounds with 9.0 boards per contest.

Nikos Gkikas of Panionios dished a round-best 9 assists. Arnas Velicka of Neptunas tops the assists charts with 7.6 assists per night.

Two players – Shavar Reynolds of the London Lions and Jordan Usher of Turk Telekom Ankara – had 4 steals in this round, the best mark of any player. Dogus Ozdemiroglu of Turk Telekom is the EuroCup’s leader in steals with 2.5 per game.

Kaodirichi Akobundu-Ehiogu of Manresa and Chris Horton of Umana Reyer Venice both had a round-high 3 blocks. Austin Wiley narrowly leads the charts in blocks with an average of 1.64 per game, just ahead of Akobundu-Ehiogu (1.61 per game).

Austin Wiley (17 points, 12 rebounds), Anthony Lamb (10 points, 12 rebounds), Bahcesehir College Istanbul’s Tyler Cavanaugh (12 points, 10 rebounds) and Besiktas GAIN Istanbul’s Ante Zizic (10 points, 11 rebounds) were the four players to record a double-double in Round 13.

heroMedia.summary: The Latvian sharpshooter shined bright with a career-high 33 points

linkedIds.text: Veolia Towers Hamburg v Neptunas Klaipeda (2026-01-07T18:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
Neptunas Klaipeda secured a 94-98 win over Veolia Towers Hamburg on Wednesday night, improving to 6-7 in Group A and taking a vital step in the race for a top-six finish.

Coming off two straight losses, the Lithuanian side knew a win was essential to keep its playoff hopes alive and stayed composed in a back-and-forth game before delivering late in the final quarter.

Rihards Lomazs was the driving force for the Lithuanian side, posting a career-high 33 points and 6 assists for a game-best 36 PIR. Held to just 8 points at halftime, he caught fire after the break and carried the offense when it mattered most.

‘‘For sure, those stops we got in the end, it was crucial. And we kind of stuck together the whole game,’’ said the Latvian guard. ‘‘I try to do whatever the defense gives and sometimes it was a pull-up, sometimes it was a drive, sometimes a three-pointer, so basketball is a game of reads and yeah, probably I did the right reads today… If you want to compete in the playoffs, you have to win on the road.’’

Neptunas stayed level in a fast-paced game, with both teams trading baskets in a balanced first half. The visitors' ability to limit their turnovers proved decisive, and a crucial turnover from Hamburg with 18 seconds remaining allowed Neptunas to pull ahead and close out the win.

Bench production proved to be another key factor for Neptunas. Zane Waterman gave the visitors a lift in the second quarter and finished with 15 points, while Harrison Cleary delivered in the final period, knocking down key three-pointers on his way to 16 points. Arnas Velicka also provided multiple scoring options, adding another layer to Neptunas’ offense.

‘‘Overall, I don’t think that today was a bad energy and bad mindset,’’ said coach Gediminas Petrauskas after the game. ‘‘Our problem was shooting. We created shots, we missed, even the layups. I felt that our team is a little bit nervous, we feel the pressure, we must win, we need to win, and it felt a little bit on our shoulders.’’

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by BC Neptūnas Klaipėda (@bcneptunas)

Hamburg, chasing its first win of the season, came close behind a red-hot Ross Williams, who carried the offense and kept his team in the game. The American guard posted a career-high 32 points and 5 rebounds for a PIR of 34. He made 5 three-pointers and was 8-of-8 in the paint.

Zacharie Perrin also contributed heavily, especially in the second half, finishing with 16 points and making his presence felt in the paint.

Next up, Neptunas will look to carry the momentum back home as it faces Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana, while Hamburg will continue its search for a first win of the season on the road at U-BT Cluj-Napoca.

## Wednesday, January 7

### [Turk Telekom Ankara 92-86 Dolomiti Energia Trento](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/game-center/2025-26/turk-telekom-ankara-dolomiti-energia-trento/U2025/130/)

Turk Telekom defeated Trento 92-86 on Wednesday night at Ankara Spor Salonu to snap a two-game losing streak and remain in thick of the battle for a top-two spot in Group B. Kris Bankston led six Turk Telekom scorers in double figures with 14 points and Kyle Alexander added 13 in lifting their team's record to 8-5.

Trento outshot Turk Telekom from all distances, but allowed 14 offensive rebounds – which the hosts converted into 18 second-chance points, and committed 22 turnovers, which together were too much to overcome. Matas Jogela and Peyton Aldridge scored 16 apiece to pace Trento, which saw its four-game winning streak end as it fell to 7-6.

Neither team led by more than 5 points in the first half, which finished with Turk Telekom up 47-43. Uros Trifunovic heated up with 10 points in the third quarter, including a three that gave the hosts a double-digit lead, 69-57. Khalif Battle scored the first 5 points of the fourth quarter to get Trento within 69-64, but no other Trento player scored in the first 5 and a half minutes of the quarter as Turk Telekom rebuilt a double-digit lead. Jogela late threes were not enough for the visitors.

### [Lietkabelis Panevezys 73-86 Cosea JL Bourg-en-Bresse](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/game-center/2025-26/lietkabelis-panevezys-cosea-jl-bourg-en-bresse/U2025/128/)

Bourg became the first team to clinch a berth in the postseason on Wednesday after it defeated Lietkabelis 73-86 at Kalnapilio Arena and later results went its way. Bourg improved to 11-2 in the standings and is one a three-game winning streak. Meanwhile, Lietkabelis dropped to 3-10 as its already-slim chances to reach the playoffs suffered another blow.

TaJuan Agee led the winners with 19 points. Kevin Kokila added 18 while Ricky Lindo had 11 for Bourg. Jamel Morris paced Lietkabelis with 22 points, Michael Flowers scored 17, Paulius Danusevicius had 14 and Fardaws Aimaq pulled down 12 rebounds for Lietkabelis, which trailed almost all the way but managed to get within 58-60 with less than 8 minutes remaining. Kokila, Agee and Assemian Moulare stepped up in crunch time to make sure Bourg returned home with a good-as-gold road victory.

Panionios ended its 11-game losing slide by beating Buducnost 97-85 on Wednesday evening at National Athletic Center Makis Liougas. On his debut as the team’s new coach, Nenad Markovic saw a much-improved Panionios trade blows with Buducnost in the first half, as the guests took a 43-44 edge at halftime. Then the hosts exploded for 28 points in the third quarter and established a double-figure lead that they maintained throughout. 

Having scored a season-high 97 points against the EuroCup's fourth-best defense, Panionios improved its record to 2-11. Meanwhile, Buducnost suffered its third straight road loss, dropping to 8-5. 

Giorgos Tsalmpouris led all scorers with 18 points on 4-for-7 shooting from deep. Joe Thomasson stood out on his debut by recording 16 points, 6 assists and 4 rebounds. Nate Watson netted 14 points and Jackson Kreuser bagged 13. Nikola Tanaskovic paced Buducnost with 17 points and Yogi Ferrell scored 15 in a losing effort.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by 🏀 Panionios Basketball 🏀 (@panioniosgssbc)

### [Slask Wroclaw 72-115 Hapoel Midtown Jerusalem](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/game-center/2025-26/slask-wroclaw-hapoel-midtown-jerusalem/U2025/121/)

Jerusalem’s winning streak moved to 10 games on Wednesday when it defeated Slask Wroclaw and broke multiple records at Hala Orbita. Setting the pace from opening tip, Yonatan Alon’s men cruised to a 72-115 victory which improved their record to 11-2 for the EuroCup campaign.

The hosts’ 64-point first half bested their previous club half scoring record of 61, then with 2 minutes on the clock, they improved their previous game single game record of 112. They did not take their foot off the gas, also shattering their previous score differential record by securing the 43-point victory.

Austin Wiley led the way with a double-double, recording a career-high 18 points and 12 boards. Cassius Winston led the way in scoring, finishing the game with 22 of his own, while Jared Harper scored steadily to earn 17 points and 8 assists. Justin Smith, Anthony Lamb and Isaiah Mobley also finished the game in double digits. Ajdin Penava and Jakub Niziol netted 15 each in defeat.

### [Veolia Towers Hamburg 94-98 Neptunas Kleipeda](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/game-center/2025-26/veolia-towers-hamburg-neptunas-klaipeda/U2025/124/)

Neptunas claimed a hard-fought 94-98 road win at Hamburg's Inselpark Arena on Wednesday night. The result lifted Neptunas to 6-7 in Group A, while Hamburg fell to 0-13. Played at a fast pace, both teams matched each other early on, with a balanced first half setting the tone. Neptunas’s ability to limit turnovers proved decisive, and in a tight finish, Hamburg’s late turnover with 20 seconds remaining allowed the visitors to pull ahead and close out the game.

For Neptunas, Rihards Lomazs led the way with a career-high 33 points and 6 assists, while Harrison Cleary added 16, Zane Waterman finished with 15, and Arnas Velicka contributed 11 points and 4 assists. Ross Williams posted a career-high 32 points and 5 rebounds for Hemburg, Zacharie Perrin added 16 points and 5 rebounds, Leonard Thorpe recorded 14 and 4 assists, and Martin Breunig finished with 12.

### [Umana Reyer Venice 90-83 U-BT Cluj-Napoca](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/game-center/2025-26/umana-reyer-venice-u-bt-cluj-napoca/U2025/125/)

Reyer remained a powerhouse at home with an important 90-83 victory over Cluj to snatch the head-to-head tiebreaker between them. Reyer put together a 13-5 run late in the third quarter for a 12-point lead and made it hold up to extend its winning streak to three games and improve to 7-6 including, 5-1 at the Palasport Taliercio. 

Stefan Nikolic poured in a career-high 23 points to lead the way for Reyer with RJ Cole picking up 18 points and 8 rebounds. Cluj had won its last two games but fell to 7-6 despite Trey Woodbury and Mitch Creek both scoring 18 points and Dusan Miletic tallying 16 points with 9 rebounds.

### [London Lions 70-78 NINERS Chemnitz](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/game-center/2025-26/london-lions-niners-chemnitz/U2025/126/)

NINERS grabbed its fourth consecutive victory in Group B by downing London 70-78 at the Copper Box Arena in the British capital on Wednesday. Chemnitz improved to 7-6 in the standings and has a two-win gap over the Lions, which dropped to 5-8. Chemnitz also claimed the head-to-head tie-break advantage after London had recorded a 67-68 road victory against the NINERS in Round 4.

Kostja Mushidi paced the winners with 22 points and 5 assists. Kevin Yebo added 17 points, 6 rebounds and 5 assists, Corey Davis had 12 points while Yordan Minchev got 11 for Chemnitz. Tarik Phillip and Shavar Reynolds led the Lions with 15 points apiece. Deane Williams added 13 while Karolis Lukosiunas got 10 for the hosts, who trailed 68-69 with 3 minutes left. Davis buried a critical three-pointer and followed Mushidi's layup with and driving basket that sealed the outcome, 70-76, in the next-to-last minute.

## Tuesday, January 6

### [Besiktas GAIN Istanbul 97-76 ratiopharm ulm](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/game-center/2025-26/besiktas-gain-istanbul-ratiopharm-ulm/U2025/127/)

Besiktas remained the only undefeated team at home in the EuroCup by rolling past Ulm 97-76 to snap a two-game losing streak. Besiktas took control of the game with a 23-4 run spanning the middle two quarters to improve to 9-4 overall and 7-0 at home. 

Vitto Brown paced five Besiktas players in double figures with 23 points while Jonah Mathews scored 18 points with 4 assists and Ante Zizic tallied 10 points and 11 rebounds in the win. Ulm continues to struggle with its sixth loss in the last seven games for a 5-8 record. Christopher Ledlum paced the guests with 17 points.

Manresa moved ahead of its opposition on Tuesday night at Pavello Nou Congost, holding on to win by 14 and move to 7-6 for the season. The home team's best work was done in the first half, in which they smashed their single-half scoring record by 63 in the opening 20 minutes. Despite a buoyant fourth quarter comeback attempt, Aris couldn’t quite come back from a sizeable 32-point deficit, so slips below the hosts with a 6-7 EuroCup record.

Diego Ocampo’s team scored by committee on their way to victory, with Retin Obasohan leading the way with 18 points from 7-10 shooting. Ferran Bassas, Agustin Ubal and Gerard Fernandez all finished the game with a total of 14, while Marcis Steinbergs and Alex Reyes reached 12 apiece.

Bahcesehir secured a 59-67 road win over Cedevita Olimpija at Arena Stozice in a closely contested Group A matchup, breaking the tie between the two sides to move to 9-4 in the standings, while Cedevita dropped to 8-5. linkedIds.text: Panionios Cosmorama Travel Athens v Buducnost VOLI Podgorica (2026-01-07T17:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
Almost 100 days had passed since [Panionios Cosmorama Travel Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/teams/panionios-cosmorama-travel-athens/nio/) celebrated its first and only BKT EuroCup win of the season. Heading into Wednesday's clash against a strong [Buducnost VOLI Podgorica](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/teams/buducnost-voli-podgorica/bud/) side, Panionios had 23 losses in 24 games across all competitions, including 11 consecutive defeats in Europe.

Nenad Markovic had shortly arrived to become the team’s fifth coach of the season in a desperate pursuit of the new-coach bounce. His appointment was followed by the arrival of experienced guard [Joe Thomasson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/joe-thomasson/012095/), who inspired Panionios’s best performance this term.

The Greek side pulled off an upset against a Buducnost team that came into the game as the competition’s fourth-best defense with an average of 78.6 points conceded per game. Not only did Panionios reach its average of 75 points scored per game with 9 minutes left on the clock, but Markovic’s side exploded to a season-high point tally in a 97-85 triumph at home.

content.title: Panionios Cosmorama Travel Athens-Buducnost VOLI Podgorica | Round 13 Highlights | 2025-26 BKT EuroCup

content.text:
“We played against one of the contenders for the EuroCup,” Markovic said in the post-game press conference. “They show every year great performance in the ABA League and EuroCup. It wasn’t easy for the guys, because third coach, third philosophy, everything changed. But they responded fantastic, they brought great energy, they stayed together.”

The hosts bettered all their scoring averages by going 20-for-32 (62.5% 2FG) from inside the arc, 14-for-27 (51.9% 3FG) from deep and 15-for-17 (88.2%) from the free-throw line. They also dished as many as 25 assists and outrebounded Buducnost 31-30.

“I think that this victory is important to make them understand what they need to do as a team to win the games. And we showed it today,” Markovic added. “We played much better defense, we fought, we stayed together in the difficult moments, we controlled the rebounds. Many players made contributions and I think that’s the way for us.”

The team’s confidence on the court was embodied by Thomasson, who hit the ground running at Panionios. Introduced in the starting five by Markovic, the 32-year-old guard embraced the role of the leader from the off.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by 🏀 Panionios Basketball 🏀 (@panioniosgssbc)

He helped his team dictate the tempo on offense and hustled from start to finish on defense, pulling off a complete display. In fact, Thomasson ended the game on 16 points, having gone 4-for-6 on field goals and 6-for-6 from the charity stripe. He also bagged 6 assists and 4 rebounds, as well as 1 steal and a block.

“Thomasson is a player who played in winning situations before in Gran Canaria, in Tofas, in Manresa,” Markovic noted. “And he has this mentality to put these guys together and make them understand that he came here to win, not to lose. I think it’s good that a player with his experience and his impact can help other players too.”

Panionios's performance against Buducnost could serve as the turning point for a team that were desperate for a spark, which has been provided by Markovic and Thomasson.

Following such a boost in morale, they will look to build some momentum and find the consistency that will allow them to end the season on a positive note by bouncing back from adversity in this final stretch of the 2025-26 campaign.

heroMedia.title: Jerusalem shatters records in historic 115-72 win over Slask
heroMedia.summary: Austin Wiley’s career night headlines a dominant team effort as Jerusalem sets new marks for points, margin, and first-half scoring

linkedIds.text: Slask Wroclaw v Hapoel Midtown Jerusalem (2026-01-07T18:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
“I feel great,” said Austin Wiley, moments after he helped his Hapoel Midtown Jerusalem team break multiple club records and extend its winning streak to 10 games, defeating Slask Wroclaw 72-115.

The visitors arrived at Hala Orbita on a mission, controlling the pace from opening tip and finishing the opening half with 64 points, besting the previous club record of 61 for a EuroCup game. Despite the efforts of Ainars Bagatskis’s men, Jerusalem could not be slowed down and at the final buzzer, had passed its single-game total point record of 112, which stood for 18 years. To top off the monumental performance, Yonatan Alon’s team also recorded the largest margin of victory in club history.

The emphatic result punctuates a dominant run for the club, which improved its winning streak to 10 consecutive games, climbing to 11-2 for the campaign. There was not a facet of the game in which the road team did not overwhelm its Polish counterparts. Jerusalem outrebounded Slask by 13 boards, logged 15 more team assists and kept its turnovers in single digits. It shot the lights out, too, making 16 of 29 efforts from beyond the arc.

Wiley shone with a truly impressive double-double, recording a career-high 18 points and 12 boards in less than 15 minutes on the floor. Cassius Winston led the way in scoring, finishing the game with 22 of his own.  Jared Harper scored steadily to earn 17 points and 8 assists. Justin Smith, Anthony Lamb and Isaiah Mobley also finished the game in double digits, with Lamb coralling 12 rebounds.

“It was a great team effort, a great win, everybody contributed,” said Wiley. “I am most proud that we held each other accountable throughout the game, even when we went up big, we kept the intensity and held each other accountable.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Hapoel Jerusalem BC (@jerusalembasket)

For Coach Bagatskis and his team, the result is a major blow to their playoff hopes, as it slid to 4-9 for the season. He hopes that this demoralizing defeat will result in a lesson learned.

“In the second half, we let them do whatever they want,” said Bagatskis. “We did not use the fouls, did not use our bodies. I hope we take this game as a lesson for the next games. The basketball starts with the mindset you put in your head. You have to play physical, aggressive, even dirty if you want to compete with these guys.”

Slask will meet Umana Reyer Venice next Wednesday, as it hopes to bounce back from the crushing loss. On that same night, the seemingly unstoppable Jerusalem faces a tough test against Aris Thessaloniki Betsson.

heroMedia.title: Bourg dominates inside to outlast Lietkabelis, clinches postseason berth
heroMedia.summary: Kokila and Agee combine for 37 points as Bourg executes game plan to perfection

linkedIds.text: Lietkabelis Panevezys v Cosea JL Bourg-en-Bresse (2026-01-07T17:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
Cosea JL Bourg-en-Bresse executed its game plan to perfection, using size and physicality to beat Lietkabelis Panevezys 73–86 at Kalnapilio Arena on Wednesday. The win lifted Bourg to 11–2 in Group B and by the end of the evening, it also meant that Bourg became the first team to clinch a spot in the postseason. Bourg now has a two-game cushion over second-place Besiktas GAIN Istanbul - and a six-win lead over seventh-place London Lions - with five games left in the round-robin phase.

From the start, Bourg imposed its will in the paint. A 0–10 run capped by 6 points from Kevin Kokila gave the visitors a 12–22 lead they never relinquished. Kokila was unstoppable early, scoring 14 points on 6-of-8 shooting and grabbing 4 rebounds by halftime.

“Physicality was the key,” Kokila said. “It is important to start the new year with a win in the EuroCup. We have to continue because the season is long.”

TaJuan Agee provided a big boost off the bench, playing both center and power forward. At the break, he had 7 points and 3 rebounds, and he kept delivering after halftime. Agee finished with 19 points, Kokila added 18, and together they combined to make 12-of-17 two-pointers and 13-of-14 free throws.

Lietkabelis fought back to within 58–60 early in the fourth quarter, but Bourg found its X-factor: Assemian Moulare, scoreless until then, erupted for 9 points in the final 6 minutes as Bourg built a decisive 66–81 margin. Bourg also caught fire from deep, hitting 4-of-8 triples in the fourth after going 2-of-16 through three quarters.

Coach Frederic Fauthoux praised his team’s execution: “It was a very good victory, because it’s never easy to win here. We played to our strengths, with great energy and intensity. We played very good defense tonight, and a lot of times we secured our rebounds, and that was the key.”

content.title: Agee, Kokila shine in Panavezys
content.description: Kevin Kokila and Tajuan Agee led Cosea JL Bourg-en-Bresse to a 73-86 road win over Lietkabelis Panevezys

content.text:
Rebounding was indeed crucial. Lietkabelis had dominated the boards in its last two EuroCup games, but Bourg matched its physicality, grabbing 35 rebounds and winning key stretches, outrebounding Lietkabelis 4-8 in the first quarter and 7-11 in the fourth. Bourg also excelled in efficiency: 23-of-37 two-pointers (62.2%), 22-of-24 free throws (91.7%), 18 assists, and just 6 turnovers. Darius McGhee scored only the first basket of the game, but contributed 4 rebounds and 5 assists with zero turnovers.

Despite 15 offensive rebounds, Lietkabelis struggled inside, making just 16-of-42 two-pointers (38.1%). Bourg now turns its attention to a pivotal showdown against Besiktas on January 14.

heroMedia.title: Captaining a team to the EuroLeague is main objective for Jerusalem's Yovel Zoosman
heroMedia.summary: Yovel Zoosman would like to return to the EuroLeague as the leader of his team

content.text:
[Yovel Zoosman](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/yovel-zoosman/006625/) has already played six years in the EuroLeague – four full seasons with Maccabi Tel Aviv and two more with ALBA Berlin. But leading [Hapoel Midtown Jerusalem](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/teams/hapoel-midtown-jerusalem/jer/) to the continent's elite competition would be something different entirely, especially as captain.

Zoosman and Jerusalem are just five victories away from playing in the EuroLeague for the first time since the 2000-01 season, with their sights set on winning the BKT EuroCup.

He has been a key part of Jerusalem's success this season, averaging 7.9 points on 45.5% three-point shooting, 3.9 rebounds, 1.3 assists and 1.3 steals.

The 27-year-old is in his third year with Jerusalem since returning home following his stint in Germany with Berlin, and reaching the EuroLeague has been the main goal from the very start for the Kfar Saba native.

Jerusalem was still playing in the FIBA Champions League in 2023-24 – having reached the final of the competition a year earlier – but club management had begun talking openly about getting to the EuroLeague, and that was a major draw for Zoosman.

"I was playing at ALBA and I had one more year on my contract. I tried to think about what was best for my career going forward: playing in the EuroLeague at ALBA, or trying to get a team to the EuroLeague. The team's focus was going to the EuroLeague, but not in the sense of 'we want to go next year.' It has to be a plan. You can't just state something and not back it up – bringing the right pieces, the right players, doing it day in and day out," Zoosman told David Hein.

content.title: Yovel Zoosman's best plays in 2025-26
content.description: The top plays of Hapoel Midtown Jerusalem forward Yovel Zoosman in the 2025-26 BKT EuroCup regular season.

content.text:
The allure of helping Jerusalem return to the EuroLeague was simply too hard to pass up.

"I'm dreaming about this moment because I've played in the EuroLeague before with two other teams. But to qualify for the EuroLeague and be the captain of this team is something very unique – it was one of the main reasons I came to Jerusalem from ALBA Berlin. I came from the EuroLeague and felt this team had real aspirations about getting there."

Zoosman believes the team has everything it needs to take the EuroCup crown. But the key to finishing the job, he says, will be on the defensive end.

"We know we have a very talented offensive team, but the DNA we need is a defensive mentality first – because that's what wins championships, that's what wins games at the end of the day. We know we can score the ball very well, so we need to do the job defensively. We have a bunch of hard workers, not talkers. That's very important for a team," said Zoosman.

Jerusalem is not a loud team – much like its captain. Part of that comes from being slightly overlooked in Israel, where the conversation tends to revolve around the country's two EuroLeague clubs, Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv and Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv.

"They always talk about the two EuroLeague teams and put us underneath them. They don't talk about us much, but we like to focus on ourselves. That's very important to our mentality as a team. We don't like to talk. We don't like to brag. We just like to play basketball, enjoy ourselves and compete to the best of our abilities," he said.

content.title: Yovel Zoosman and Anthony Lamb come together during a game this season.

content.text:
Zoosman knows what he has in Jerusalem, and last May he made his commitment clear, signing a three-year extension through 2028.

"It was very simple for me: being on a team I love and being its captain – I have more responsibility than I've ever had before, and I really like that. It says a lot about a person, leading by example and trying to do it well," Zoosman said.

"I know I have a special role – with the community, with the team, with the crowd, and with myself – to never settle. Just keep pushing to be the best player I can be, so that when I retire, I can look back and say: you did well. You never shied away from the hard work."

And he simply wants to see it through.

"We have an amazing team. I want to be part of it. We started something special, and I want to see it to the end."

All the way back to the EuroLeague.

heroMedia.title: BKT EuroCup Eighthfinals MVP: Iverson Molinar, U-BT Cluj-Napoca

linkedIds.text: Buducnost VOLI Podgorica v U-BT Cluj-Napoca (2026-03-11T18:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Iverson Molinar](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/iverson-molinar/013926/) stepped up when [U-BT Cluj-Napoca](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/teams/u-bt-cluj-napoca/clu/) needed him most, delivering a career-best performance in the BKT EuroCup Eighthfinals to earn MVP of the Round honors. Molinar led Cluj to an 82-100 road win over Buducnost VOLI Podgorica to send his team into the quarterfinals for a date with Bahcesehir College Istanbul.

Molinar shined in the Montenegrin capital with a career-high 34 points, making 8-of-12 two-pointers, 3-of-3 threes and all 9 of his free-throw attempts. He also had 5 rebounds, 4 assists, 2 steals and drew 7 fouls for a PIR 39, also a career-best.

[DeVante' Jones](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/devante-jones/013260/) of Dolomiti Energia Trento had a 38 PIR in a 90-94 overtime win over Umana Reyer Venice. Jones netted 38 points on 6-of-12 two-pointers, 5-of-6 three-pointers and 11-of-13 free throws, while dishing 9 assists and drawing 9 fouls.

The next best PIR, 23, belonged to his Trento teammate, [Matas Jogela](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/matas-jogela/006853/), who collected 15 points, 12 rebounds and 5 assists. Rounding out the list of top performers in the eighthfinals were Cluj’s [Fatts Russell](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/daron-russell/013943/) and [Michael Devoe](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/eurocup/players/michael-jaden-devoe/013922/) of Turk Telekom Ankara, each with a 22 PIR. Russell had 20 points, 7 assists, 4 rebounds and 2 steals in a win over Buducnost, while Devoe posted 16 points, 7 assists and 2 rebounds in a 98-81 victory over BAXI Manresa.

### DeVante' Jones, Trento, 38 points

Jones led all players in the eighthfinals with 38 points. Cluj’s Russell remains the EuroCup’s top scorer with 19.7 points per game.

Jogela had 12 rebounds, the most in the eighthfinal games. Fardaws Aimaq of Lietkabelis Panevezys is this season’s top rebounder with 9.6 boards per game.

Devoe and Russell each dished 7 assists, an eighthfinals best. Neptunas Klaipeda’s Arnas Velicka is the assists king with 7.7 dimes per game. 

Dogus Ozdemiroglu of Turk Telekom Ankara had 3 steals, more than any other player in the eighthfinal games. Ozdemiroglu leads the steals charts with 2.1 per contest.

Matthew Hurt of Cedevita Olimpija Ljubljana led all players with 3 blocks. Manresa’s Kaodirichi Akobundu-Ehiogu has 1.6 blocks per game to top the standings.  

The one double-double across the four eighthfinal games belonged to Jogela, who had 15 points and 12 rebounds.

heroMedia.title: DeVante' Jones explodes in overtime as Trento edges Reyer in eighthfinal classic
heroMedia.summary: The 27-year-old guard scored a career-high 38 points - including 11 of his team’s 13 points in overtime

linkedIds.text: Umana Reyer Venice v Dolomiti Energia Trento (2026-03-11T19:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
No Group A team had won more home games than Umana Reyer Venice during the 2025-26 BKT EuroCup Regular Season, with Coach Neven Spahija’s side turning Palasport Taliercio into a genuine fortress by winning eight of their nine encounters.

Dolomiti Energia Trento fought hard to find a way to breach it on Wednesday night and it finally managed to do so through a sublime DeVante' Jones. Riding on their momentum following a 9-2 run in the second half of the campaign, the visitors managed to beat Reyer 90-94 in an all-Italian duel that was decided by Jones in overtime.

The Trento guard went 6-for-12 from inside the arc, 5-for-6 from deep and 11-for-13 from the charity stripe to bag a career-high 38 points, which he paired with 6 assists. Eleven of those points came in overtime, when he put in a show to single-handedly clinch a spot in the quarterfinals of the competition.

Jones hit the ground running by netting 11 of his team’s 26 points in the first quarter before finishing the first half on 21. Reyer found ways to contain him in the second half, when he only managed 6 points. In fact, after trading blows for most of the third and fourth quarters, Reyer went on a 7-2 run that gave it a 79-74 lead heading into crunch time.

However, a timeout called by Massimo Cancellieri would serve as a wake-up that saw the momentum shift in his team’s favor, as Trento ended regulation with a 2-9 run that sent the game to overtime.

“I remember the timeout, down by 5, that we did a 2-9 [run] in 1 and a half minute,” Cancellieri recalled after the game. “That for us means a lot. When we are solid and when we are together, we are connected, we can bother everybody. Today we did it in Venice.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by BKT EuroCup (@eurocup)

Then, it was Jones's moment to step up.

He made a couple of free throws to open the final 5 minutes before draining 3 straight triples for an 88-92 lead. Jones was involved in all of Trento’s 13 points in overtime by scoring 11 and setting up DJ Steward for a dunk in transition that sealed the outcome with just a few seconds left on the clock. An extraordinary outing by an MVP candidate.

“Without [my teammates] I wouldn’t be in this position individually, we wouldn’t be in this position as a team,” a humble Jones said after the game. “I lay on them every day, in practice we go hard. Traveling, we are always together. I think this game shows who we are.”

content.title: Game News

linkedId.text: Umana Reyer Venice v Dolomiti Energia Trento (2026-03-11T19:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
Having reached the playoffs for the first time in a decade, Trento will look to replicate a run that saw it feature in the semifinals back in 2015-16, when it eliminated Zaragoza and Milan before losing to Strasbourg.

“We are very, very happy,” Cancellieri said in the post-game press conference. “This is a historic win for us as a group, for the club. We did an amazing job today. It was super tough to face Venice, face this team in Venice.”

Trento is now set to take on a Besiktas GAIN Istanbul side that remains unbeaten at home with nine victories in as many games. But with Jones leading the charge, the Italian side proved on Wednesday night that it has what it takes to win anywhere.
